```{r}
install.packages(c("readxl", "ggplot2", "dplyr", "tidyr", "corrplot", 
                   "extRemes", "mgcv", "gridExtra"))
```

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(corrplot)
library(extRemes)
library(mgcv)
library(gridExtra)
```

```{r}
df <- read_excel("envDataset_12h_10x10_with_mask - Copy.xlsx", sheet = "in")

# Quick check
dim(df)                    # should be ~30,827 rows × 151 columns
table(df$basin)            # WP, SP, SI, NI counts
table(df$TC_genesis)       # 0 vs 1
head(df[, c("basin", "sea_surface_temperature", "mean_sea_level_pressure", "TC_genesis")])
```
```{r}
df %>% 
  group_by(basin) %>% 
  summarise(
    Total_rows = n(),
    TC_genesis_1 = sum(TC_genesis == 1),
    TC_genesis_0 = sum(TC_genesis == 0),
    Percent_TCs = round(mean(TC_genesis)*100, 1)
  )
```

```{r}
df <- df %>%
  mutate(surface_wind = sqrt(wind_u^2 + wind_v^2))

# Key variables we care about
key_vars <- c("sea_surface_temperature", "mean_sea_level_pressure", 
              "relative_humidity", "vorticity_relative", 
              "vertical_wind_shear_between_200_hPa_and_850_hPa", "surface_wind")

# 1. Boxplots by basin + TC_genesis (overlaps visible here)
for (var in key_vars) {
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = basin, y = !!sym(var), fill = factor(TC_genesis))) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    labs(title = paste(var, "by Basin and TC_genesis"),
         subtitle = "Overlap = similar environmental conditions across basins") +
    theme_minimal()
  print(p)
  ggsave(paste0("boxplot_", var, ".png"), p, width = 8, height = 5)
}
```

```{r}
tc_only <- df %>% filter(TC_genesis == 1)

# 1. Summary table per basin (easy to copy into your report)
summary_table <- tc_only %>%
  group_by(basin) %>%
  summarise(
    n_TCs = n(),
    Mean_wind = round(mean(surface_wind, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Max_wind = round(max(surface_wind, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Mean_SST = round(mean(sea_surface_temperature, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Mean_MSLP = round(mean(mean_sea_level_pressure, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Mean_shear_200_850 = round(mean(`vertical_wind_shear_between_200_hPa_and_850_hPa`, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
  )
print(summary_table)
```

```{r}
# 2. Density plot of intensity (surface_wind) by basin – shows differences
p_density <- ggplot(tc_only, aes(x = surface_wind, fill = basin)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Cyclone Intensity (surface wind) – genesis=1 only",
       subtitle = "WP usually strongest, SI weakest") +
  theme_minimal()
print(p_density)
ggsave("intensity_density_genesis1.png", p_density, width = 8, height = 5)
```

```{r}
# Create pressure proxy (higher value = stronger storm, exactly like wind)
tc_only <- tc_only %>%
  mutate(pressure_intensity = -mean_sea_level_pressure)

# Density plot for pressure (parallel to your wind plot)
p_pressure <- ggplot(tc_only, aes(x = pressure_intensity, fill = basin)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Cyclone Intensity (Pressure proxy: -MSLP) – genesis=1 only",
       subtitle = "Higher value = lower central pressure = stronger storm",
       x = "Pressure intensity (-MSLP)") +
  theme_minimal()
print(p_pressure)
ggsave("pressure_intensity_density_genesis1.png", p_pressure, width = 8, height = 5)
```
```{r}
summary_both <- tc_only %>%
  group_by(basin) %>%
  summarise(
    n_TCs                  = n(),
    Mean_wind              = round(mean(surface_wind, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Max_wind               = round(max(surface_wind, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Mean_MSLP              = round(mean(mean_sea_level_pressure, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Min_MSLP               = round(min(mean_sea_level_pressure, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Mean_pressure_intensity = round(mean(pressure_intensity, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
  )
print(summary_both)
```

```{r}
wp <- df %>% 
  filter(basin == "WP", TC_genesis == 1) %>% 
  mutate(pressure_intensity = -mean_sea_level_pressure)

print(paste("WP has", nrow(wp), "TC genesis events"))
```
```{r}
wp_summary <- wp %>% 
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    Mean_wind = round(mean(surface_wind), 2),
    Max_wind = round(max(surface_wind), 2),
    Mean_MSLP = round(mean(mean_sea_level_pressure), 2),
    Min_MSLP = round(min(mean_sea_level_pressure), 2)
  )
print(wp_summary)
```
```{r}
# Create blocks (~1 year at 12-hourly) — adjust divisor if needed
wp <- wp %>% 
  mutate(block = floor((row_number()-1)/730) + 1)

block_max_wp <- wp %>% 
  group_by(block) %>% 
  summarise(
    max_wind = max(surface_wind),
    max_pressure_int = max(pressure_intensity)
  ) %>% ungroup()
```

```{r}
wp <- df %>% 
  filter(basin == "WP", TC_genesis == 1) %>% 
  mutate(
    pressure_intensity = -mean_sea_level_pressure,
    block = floor((row_number() - 1) / 20) + 1   # ← changed from 730 to 20
  )
```

```{r}
block_max_wp <- wp %>% 
  group_by(block) %>% 
  summarise(
    max_wind = max(surface_wind, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_pressure_int = max(pressure_intensity, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>% 
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
print(paste("Number of blocks created:", nrow(block_max_wp)))   # should be ~43–44
print(summary(block_max_wp))
```


```{r}
library(extRemes)
# Now fit GEV — this will work
gev_wind <- fevd(block_max_wp$max_wind, type = "GEV")
gev_press <- fevd(block_max_wp$max_pressure_int, type = "GEV")

print("=== GEV Wind (WP) ==="); print(gev_wind)
print("=== GEV Pressure Intensity (WP) ==="); print(gev_press)
```
```{r}
rl_wind   <- return.level(gev_wind,   return.period = c(10, 50, 100))
rl_press  <- return.level(gev_press,  return.period = c(10, 50, 100))

print("Wind return levels (m/s):"); print(rl_wind)
print("Pressure intensity return levels (-hPa):"); print(rl_press)
```
```{r}
# Add covariates (mean per block)
cov_wp <- wp %>% 
  group_by(block) %>% 
  summarise(
    SST = mean(sea_surface_temperature, na.rm = TRUE),
    shear_200_850 = mean(`vertical_wind_shear_between_200_hPa_and_850_hPa`, na.rm = TRUE),
    RH = mean(relative_humidity, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>% ungroup()

block_max_cov <- left_join(block_max_wp, cov_wp, by = "block")

# Non-stationary GEV (location depends on covariates)
gev_ns_wind <- fevd(block_max_cov$max_wind, data = block_max_cov,
                    location.fun = ~ SST + shear_200_850 + RH, type = "GEV")

gev_ns_press <- fevd(block_max_cov$max_pressure_int, data = block_max_cov,
                     location.fun = ~ SST + shear_200_850 + RH, type = "GEV")

print("=== Non-stationary GEV Wind (WP) ==="); print(gev_ns_wind)
print("=== Non-stationary GEV Pressure (WP) ==="); print(gev_ns_press)
```

```{r}
#GLM & GAM for surface wind (Gamma family)
glm_wind <- glm(surface_wind ~ sea_surface_temperature + 
                `vertical_wind_shear_between_200_hPa_and_850_hPa` + 
                relative_humidity + vorticity_relative,
                data = wp, family = Gamma(link = "log"))

gam_wind <- gam(surface_wind ~ s(sea_surface_temperature) + 
                s(`vertical_wind_shear_between_200_hPa_and_850_hPa`) + 
                s(relative_humidity) + s(vorticity_relative),
                data = wp, family = Gamma(link = "log"), method = "REML")

summary(glm_wind)
summary(gam_wind)
plot(gam_wind, pages = 1, main = "GAM smooths - Wind intensity (WP)")   # visual proof of non-linearity
```
```{r}
# Same models for pressure intensity
glm_press <- glm(pressure_intensity ~ sea_surface_temperature + 
                 `vertical_wind_shear_between_200_hPa_and_850_hPa` + 
                 relative_humidity + vorticity_relative,
                 data = wp, family = Gamma(link = "log"))

gam_press <- gam(pressure_intensity ~ s(sea_surface_temperature) + 
                 s(`vertical_wind_shear_between_200_hPa_and_850_hPa`) + 
                 s(relative_humidity) + s(vorticity_relative),
                 data = wp, family = Gamma(link = "log"), method = "REML")

summary(gam_press)
plot(gam_press, pages = 1, main = "GAM smooths - Pressure intensity (WP)")
What to report from the models

Look at p-values in summary(glm_…) — significant covariates = important drivers.
Look at edf (effective degrees of freedom) in summary(gam_…) — edf > 1 means non-linear (GAM wins).
The smooth plots (plot(gam_…)) are gold — screenshot them and say “Figure X shows non-linear effects of SST and shear on intensity.”

Run the code above, send me the summary(gam_wind) and summary(gam_press) output (or screenshots), and I’ll:

Write the full “Results – Covariate Effects” subsection
Interpret which variables matter most
Add a comparison table (GLM vs GAM AIC)

You now have the complete theory + code for objective 5. This will make your supervisor very happy. Paste the output whenever you run it and we’ll finish the results section today!9.4sExpert
```
